R3: | Presentation (0-5) | Motivation (0-5) | Technical skills (0-5) | Background knowledge (0-5) | Fit with lab (0-5) | Notes | Total score | | Average score
R4: Anniina | 3.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | Basic bioinformatic knowledge, would be great in setting up new tissue based methods. Knowledge of histology, TMAs and FFPE tissues is a huge plus. Has worked independently, but also shown evidence of supervising students. | 20 | | 19.25
R5: Joonas | 3.0 | 3.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | A lot of wet lab experience with relevant methods, not so much on the computational side. However, with her unusual lab comp (more clinicans, less researchers), she likely has become very independent and capable in the methods she has used. All analyses performed using QuPath and ML, not using the image data/pixel values, so methods somewhat different to ours (that is not necessarily a bad thing). | 19
R6: Maria | 3.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | Her presentation was a bit broad and not very stimulating for me. As a result, she didn't seem very motivated to me. It was not very clear what she would be interested in pursuing in the lab or how this would help her with the next steps in her career.

sult, she didn't seem very motivated to me. It was not very clear what she would be interested in pursuing in the lab or how this would help her with the next steps in her career.
Regarding technical skills, having worked with mIHC during her previous lab experience gives her a good skill set to start working in our lab. However, she might not be very skilled in the computational aspects, as the analyses she described were more superficial and relied on programs with pre-defined pipelines.
Regarding background knowledge, she has experience working on tumor-immune interactions, so she might be somewhat familiar with immunology concepts.
Altogether, she might be a good fit for our lab | 19
R7: Ziqi | 3.0 | 3.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | Rich wet lab experience with basic computational knowledge. Previous research on tumor-immune interaction is interesting and fit with our interest. The concern is she works quite independently before and she was using different approach (Qupath, etc) for image analysis than in our lab (not saying this is bad). - Overall, I think she will be very helpful on spatial-related project design (e.g. the localization of immune cells or tumor-immune interaction), but I'm not sure with the pipeline setting up of spatial proteomics. | 19
R12: Jie Bao

-related project design (e.g. the localization of immune cells or tumor-immune interaction), but I'm not sure with the pipeline setting up of spatial proteomics. | 19
R12: Jie Bao
R13: | Presentation (0-5) | Motivation (0-5) | Technical skills (0-5) | Background knowledge (0-5) | Fit with lab (0-5) | Notes | Total score | | Average score
R14: Anniina | 3.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | 4.0 | Presentation was too long and not very coherent. Has very good technical skills. Already experience with proteomics. The contract is a bit of an issue. No knowledge on immunology. Already basic knowledge about ovarian cancer. | 20 | | 20.4
R15: Joonas | 3.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | 4.0 | Presentation went overboard, tried to display way too much + too many details. Skillset is very good + has additional skills that may not be directly applicable to the position that could still be very beneficial to the lab overall. Has done a lot of mentoring, albeit mostly that of MSc students, no PhD students or bigger projects supervised. Very interested in working in an environment where the economics of research are not limiting her. The potential politics of hiring her can be an issue, but something that needs to be talked about with Tang, before making any judgement on that. | 20
R16: Ada | 4.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | I really liked the way she explained the data and made conclusions.

o be talked about with Tang, before making any judgement on that. | 20
R16: Ada | 4.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | I really liked the way she explained the data and made conclusions.
Good overlap of her background and the current projects in the lab. 
She has some image analysis skills and a good understanding of what we do. 
Experience in project coordination and mentoring
The only downside is the contract-related issues | 21
R17: Maria | 3.0 | 5.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | 4.0 | Her presentation was too long and went over time. However, it captured my attention, and I was able to follow and enjoy it. She seemed very motivated to join this lab, and excited about the science. She is familiar and comfortable with doing research in collaborative settings. 
She has very good and extensive technical skillset, which puts her in a good position to start working in the “spatial proteomics development department” in our lab, but also makes her a great asset as she can advice other students in other “departments” as well. 
She is quite familiar with cancer biology, and has worked with ovarian cancer in the past. No evidence of knowledge in immunology.
Overall, I think she is a very good fit for our lab. | 21
R18: Ziqi | 3.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | Rich wet lab experience and have many overlapping with what we are doing.

ology.
Overall, I think she is a very good fit for our lab. | 21
R18: Ziqi | 3.0 | 4.0 | 5.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | Rich wet lab experience and have many overlapping with what we are doing. No coding but have experience collaborating with computational group, which is good. Have quite strong motivation in doing research. Concerns: (1) Few immunology background (didn't mention in the presentation) (2) she is now in a 2-yr contract with Tang's lab. | 20
R23: Ahmed El Zowalaty
R24: | Presentation (0-5) | Motivation (0-5) | Technical skills (0-5) | Background knowledge (0-5) | Fit with lab (0-5) | Notes | Total score | | Average score
R25: Anniina | 3.0 | 2.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 2.0 | Extremely experimental, excited about mechanistic molecular understanding, only very basic bioinformatics, much more senior. Leadership and ability to finish and complete things (take responsibility and finish) was unclear based on the publication records and presentation. | 15 | | 15.4
R26: Joonas | 3.0 | 3.0 | 3.0 | 3.0 | 2.0 | The prototypical mouse model expert. Has done high tech mouse work, but not too many things applicable to our projects (+ most genomics analyses done by a collab instead of him). With several postdocs behind him, has very little supervision/project lead experience.

things applicable to our projects (+ most genomics analyses done by a collab instead of him). With several postdocs behind him, has very little supervision/project lead experience. Also, is a little unclear to me what his role in each of these project initiation steps was; did he do the fanciest transduction/CRISPR things or was he in more of a support role? Has not taken the steps toward leadership roles at any stage. Better fit for a less translational lab with mouse model work. | 14
R27: Ada | 3.0 | 3.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 2.0 | I did not feel particularly excited about the way he explained his prior work,
as someone with very little background on that topic it was difficult to follow, but 
also he did not tell a compelling story about the projects (his particular work in the project,
insights on the challenges, if he began some of these how did he come up with those ideas...).
He focused more on the methodology and results instead.

I feel like he has a strong experimental background but do not see
a clear thematic fit with the lab. To me it is unclear how could we 
transfer his prior experience and combine it with our work. In a future round I would like to
hear his view on this point.

Would have liked to hear more about his experience leading projects 
since it is a critical aspect that was not emphasized in the presentation.

hear his view on this point.

Would have liked to hear more about his experience leading projects 
since it is a critical aspect that was not emphasized in the presentation.
His reply to the mentoring question was to me quite vague. I would
also like to hear more about his prior experiences working with PhD and MSc students | 16
R28: Maria | 3.0 | 3.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 2.0 | Presentation was clear and straightforward. However, he didn't highlight his first author papers neither made a good link between his background and skillset and the current needs of our lab (precisely what we posted in the job ad). Even though he seem very skilled and knowledgeable, there is not much overlapping between what he could bring and what we need. I don't think he is a good fit for our lab at this time point. | 16
R29: Ziqi | 3.0 | 2.0 | 4.0 | 4.0 | 3.0 | Presentation was clear and not too long; motivation was not strong compare with other candidates. He has rich experience in both wet lab and dry lab so techinical skills shouldn't be a problem. Concerns: What I feel from his publication record and the interview is he didn't show much experience in leading projects - As he described during the interview, his PI is leading many things and give them tasks (not sure if I misunderstood) | 16
